summary: Australia's transition is led by the Australian Signals Directorate, whose guidance sets transition timelines and milestones for organisations. Australia runs one of the most compressed official timelines, with migration to be complete by 2030.
migrationTimeline:
2026 | Transition plan in place
2028 | Migration underway
2030 | Migration complete
govActors: ASD | Australian Signals Directorate, national cyber security agency (ACSC)
provenance: ASD guidance with timelines: ingested cyber.gov.au pages
dominantProcess omitted: NIST link unattested in captured text (verifier)
dominantProcess NIST restored: well-established and design-endorsed, provisional for review
Model 2026-06-14 (NIST-bloc / taker, Medium): ASD guidance aligned to the NIST suite; Five Eyes posture.
classificationBasis:
ASD guidance with timelines: ingested cyber.gov.au pages
2026/2028/2030: backend brief reference line
dominantProcess omitted: NIST link unattested in captured text (verifier)

mainRegulation:
PSPF Policy 11 (Robust ICT systems) applying the Information Security Manual | national | binding-by-market-access
Security of Critical Infrastructure Act 2018 (risk management programme) | national | binding-law
ASD/ACSC post-quantum cryptography guidance | national | guidance
obligation: Non-corporate Commonwealth entities must, under PSPF Policy 11, secure their ICT systems by applying the cyber security principles and risk-based approach of the Australian Government Information Security Manual, which the PSPF makes mandatory under the Public Governance, Performance and Accountability Act 2013; responsible entities for critical infrastructure assets must also maintain a risk management programme addressing cyber and information security hazards under the Security of Critical Infrastructure Act 2018. Neither instrument compels post-quantum migration: PQC is addressed through ASD/ACSC guidance aligned to the NIST algorithms, on an indicative timeline to 2030, with no binding obligation.
